topic: Roo Code Review 2026: Open-Source Cline Fork with Multi-Agent Mode

key_points: Multi-model AI coding agent review with pros/cons breakdown
Covers pricing tiers: free BYOK extension, Pro $20/mo, Team $99/mo, Cloud agents $5/hour
Discusses orchestrator mode and Boomerang Tasks as key differentiators
Notes shutdown announcement as critical context for new users
Open-source AI coding agent that forked from Cline to move faster
SOC 2 Type 2 compliance — rare for open-source tools
Apache 2.0 license, model-agnostic with BYOK approach
Approval fatigue cited as main UX friction point
Power-user focused tool; steeper learning curve than Cursor or Copilot
Roo Code pivots to Roomote cloud agent — thesis: IDEs are not the future of coding
Shutdown announced April 20, 2026; all products end May 15, 2026
Team recommends Cline for users who want to stay in VS Code
Extension repo will be archived after May 15 — no more updates
Roo forked from Cline to add multi-mode system: Code, Architect, Ask, Debug, Orchestrator
Cline: 58K+ stars, 5M+ installs; Roo Code: 22K+ stars, 1.2M installs (Feb 2026)
Custom Modes allow domain-specific AI personas with scoped tool permissions
Roo Code better for autonomous multi-file tasks; Cline better for step-by-step control
Orchestrator mode coordinates complex tasks by delegating to specialized sub-modes
Boomerang Tasks decompose requests into logical subtasks in separate contexts
Each subtask can use a different model (cheap model for Ask, powerful model for Code)
Enables parallel execution of sub-agents for faster multi-file task completion

stats.fact: Roo Code reached 23,300+ GitHub stars and 1.52 million active VS Code installs with 3 million cumulative downloads as of April 2026
Roo Code had 300+ active contributors in its open-source GitHub repository before the shutdown announcement
Roo Code announced shutdown on April 20, 2026; all products (extension, Cloud, Router) ceased on May 15, 2026
Cline has 58K+ GitHub stars and 5M+ installs (Feb 2026), approximately 2.5x more community adoption than Roo Code
Kilo Code, Roo Code's closest fork successor, raised $8 million in seed funding and claims 1.5M+ users after Roo's shutdown announcement
Roo Code Cloud agents were priced at $5/hour; a team using 4 hours/day would spend an extra $600/month on top of the $99/month Team plan
Roo Code Orchestrator (Boomerang) mode allows each subtask to run in its own context with a different model, significantly reducing overall inference costs for large workflows
Roo Code achieved SOC 2 Type 2 compliance — extremely rare among open-source AI coding tools

angles: Retrospective review angle: Roo Code's entire arc — from Cline fork to multi-agent innovation to shutdown — as a cautionary tale and innovation story for AI tooling in 2026
Multi-agent orchestration was Roo Code's killer feature: explain Boomerang Tasks and how per-mode model assignment cuts API costs dramatically
The shutdown pivot thesis — 'IDEs are not the future' — frames the broader question of where AI coding is heading (cloud agents vs local IDE extensions)
Kilo Code as the de facto Roo successor: who should migrate where (Kilo vs Cline vs Roomote) based on use case
Cost optimization angle: BYOK model with per-mode model routing enabled significant savings vs fixed subscription tools like Copilot
SOC 2 compliance in open source — a unique selling point that appealed to enterprise teams who needed audit trails but preferred open-source flexibility
Community velocity paradox: 300+ contributors but still shut down — the challenge of monetizing open-source developer tools even with strong adoption
keywords: open source ai coding agent vscode
best open source ai coding extension
outline: H2: What Is Roo Code? The Open-Source AI Dev Team Inside VS Code
H2: How Roo Code Forked from Cline — and Why It Mattered
H2: Key Features: The Multi-Agent Mode System Explained
H2: Boomerang Tasks: Orchestrating Complex Multi-File Workflows
H2: Custom Modes & the Community Marketplace
H2: Pricing Breakdown: Free BYOK, Cloud Agents, and Team Plans
H2: Real-World Performance: Pros and Cons from Developers
H2: The May 2026 Shutdown: What Happened and the Pivot to Roomote
H2: Where Roo Code Users Should Go Next: Kilo Code, Cline, or Roomote
